senses.definition: a large board with a smooth dark surface that is written on with chalk in schools

examples.x: The teacher wrote the lesson on the blackboard.
She erased the blackboard after class.
The blackboard was filled with math problems.
He used colored chalk to draw on the blackboard.
The classroom has a large blackboard at the front.

senses.definition: a smooth, typically dark-colored surface, usually made of slate or a similar material, used for writing or drawing with chalk

examples.x: The teacher wrote the math problem on the chalkboard for the class to solve.
She erased the notes from yesterday's lecture off the chalkboard before starting today's lesson.
The restaurant menu was written on a large chalkboard behind the counter.
The students eagerly gathered around the chalkboard to see the teacher's diagrams.

senses.definition: a large board with a smooth white surface that we can write on, especially used for teaching or presentations

examples.x: During the language class, the teacher wrote new vocabulary on the whiteboard.
During the meeting, the presenter wrote important points on the whiteboard.
The office conference room is equipped with a large whiteboard for brainstorming sessions.
The teacher wiped the whiteboard clean after the lesson.

senses.definition: a large display board that can be connected to a computer or projector, allowing users to interact with digital content using touch, stylus, or other input devices
a display board used for posting notices, announcements, or other informational materials

examples.x: The bulletin board in the classroom displayed the daily schedule.
She pinned the flyer advertising the bake sale to the bulletin board in the cafeteria.
The bulletin board in the lobby showcased upcoming events in the community.
He checked the bulletin board in the hallway for any new job openings.
The office bulletin board was filled with memos and reminders for the staff.

senses.definition: a type of thumbtack with a colored piece of plastic on one end

examples.x: She used a pushpin to hang up the calendar on the bulletin board.
The map was dotted with pushpins marking locations they planned to visit.
He secured the poster to the wall with several colorful pushpins.
The office manager used pushpins to display important notices in the break room.
Students used pushpins to attach their artwork to the display board for the school fair.

senses.definition: furniture we use for working, writing, reading, etc. that normally has a flat surface and drawers

examples.x: I have a picture frame with a family photo on my desk.
My computer is placed on the desk in the office.
The receptionist sat behind the desk, welcoming visitors.
The student sat down at his desk and opened his notebook.
The teacher placed the books on the desk.

senses.definition: a stand with a slanted top used to hold notes or books for a speaker or reader

examples.x: The professor adjusted the microphone on the lectern to make sure everyone could hear.
She placed her notes on the lectern before starting the lecture.
The lectern was positioned in the center of the stage for the keynote speaker.
He leaned on the lectern as he answered questions from the audience.

senses.definition: ‌a board connected to a wall or a piece of furniture on which books are kept

examples.x: She organized her collection of novels neatly on the bookshelf in the living room.
The bookshelf in the study was filled with textbooks and academic journals.
They installed floating bookshelves in the children's room to encourage reading.

senses.definition: a small compartment or niche used for sorting or storing items

examples.x: The mailroom had individual pigeonholes for each employee to receive their correspondence.
She organized her paperwork by placing them in different pigeonholes labeled with categories.
The librarian placed the returned books in the appropriate pigeonholes to be reshelved.
The teacher collected the students' assignments and placed them in their respective pigeonholes for grading.

senses.definition: a small closet that usually has a lock, in which valuable items and belongings could be stored

examples.x: She stored her books and backpack in her locker before heading to class.
He forgot the combination to his locker and had to ask the teacher for assistance.
Students are encouraged to keep their lockers organized and free from clutter.
The office provides lockers for employees to store their coats and personal items during work hours.
He placed his valuables in a locker before heading out.

senses.definition: a piece of office furniture with drawers in which documents can be stored

examples.x: She organized the client files in the bottom drawer of the file cabinet.
The file cabinet in the corner of the office contained important documents dating back several years.
He stored confidential paperwork securely in the locked file cabinet.
The administrative assistant retrieved the contract from the file cabinet for the meeting.
The office manager labeled each drawer of the file cabinet for easy access to different categories of files.

senses.definition: a device used for making images or videos appear on a screen, wall, or other flat surfaces

examples.x: The classroom was equipped with a state-of-the-art projector, allowing teachers to display educational videos and presentations.
They set up a projector in the backyard for an outdoor movie night, casting larger-than-life images onto a white sheet hung between trees.
The conference room had a ceiling-mounted projector for business presentations, ensuring clear visibility for all attendees.
He brought his portable projector to the camping trip, turning the side of the tent into a makeshift movie theater under the stars.
The art installation used projectors to project images onto the walls of the gallery, creating an immersive visual experience for visitors.

senses.definition: a surface, often white or gray, used to display images from a projector

examples.x: The conference room was equipped with a large projection screen for presentations.
The movie theater lowered the projection screen before starting the film.
He adjusted the height of the projection screen to ensure optimal viewing for the audience.
The school auditorium had a motorized projection screen that could be raised and lowered as needed.
The company rented a portable projection screen for the outdoor movie night event.

senses.definition: a device that projects images or text from a transparency onto a screen or wall

examples.x: The teacher used an overhead projector to display the lesson notes to the class.
The presenter set up the overhead projector for the business meeting to showcase the sales figures.
She prepared transparent slides to use with the overhead projector during her presentation.
The conference room was equipped with an overhead projector for training sessions.
The university lecture hall had multiple overhead projectors for large class presentations.

senses.definition: a large motor vehicle designed to transport students to and from school

examples.x: The school bus arrived at the bus stop to pick up the children in the morning.
She waved goodbye to her son as he boarded the school bus for his field trip.
The school bus driver reminded the students to remain seated while the bus was in motion.
Parents gathered at the bus stop to greet their children as they returned home on the school bus.
The school district implemented safety measures to ensure students' well-being while riding the school bus.

senses.definition: a mechanical or electronic device used to signal the start and end of classes or periods in a school day

examples.x: The school bell rang, signaling the end of recess and the start of the next class.
The students lined up in the hallway when they heard the school bell indicating the end of lunch.
The principal announced over the intercom that the school bell schedule would be adjusted for the upcoming assembly.
She glanced at the clock, waiting for the school bell to announce the end of the school day.
The custodian adjusted the timing of the school bell to ensure classes started promptly each morning.
